Package org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements

Class ArithmeticLiteral

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Hashable
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.Literal<ArithmeticLiteral,Term>
org.eventb.internal.pp.core.elements.ArithmeticLiteral
public final class ArithmeticLiteral extends Literal<ArithmeticLiteral,Term>
This class represents an arithmetic literal.

TODO for now, there is no normalization for arithmetic literals. This means that we have the four possible types <,≤,=,≠. Think about a nice way to normalize arithmetic literals.

      public ArithmeticLiteral substitute(Map<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map)
      Description copied from class: Literal
      Returns a copy of this literal where the substitution given in the specified map is applied.
      Specified by:
      substitute in class Literal<ArithmeticLiteral,Term>
      Parameters:
      map - the substitution map

      a copy of this literal with the applied substitution

    • equalsWithDifferentVariables

      public boolean equalsWithDifferentVariables(ArithmeticLiteral literal, HashMap<SimpleTerm,SimpleTerm> map)
      Description copied from class: Literal
      Return true if this literal is equal to the specified literal but with different instances of variables.

      Overrides:
      equalsWithDifferentVariables in class Literal<ArithmeticLiteral,Term>
      Parameters:
      literal - the literal to be checked for equality
      map - the map of correspondance for variables
      Returns:
      true if this literal is equal to the specified literal but with different instances of variables, false otherwise
      See Also:
      • Term.equalsWithDifferentVariables(Term, HashMap)

      public ArithmeticLiteral getInverse()

      Returns a copy of this literal with the opposite sign.

      a copy of this literal with the opposite sign
